label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 16

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won from reduced sprint after ROGLIC Primož crashed with WRIGHT Fred's knee hitting his bars inside final 500m.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC Primož initiated acceleration on 8.3% finishing ramp revealing GC riders willing to contest sprint stages for time gaps.
Breakaway of only two riders on 207km stage signaled peloton consensus that sprint teams would control despite steep finish.
EVENEPOEL Remco punctured within 3km protection zone, exposing vulnerability of race leader without strong team support at critical moments.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: positive then negative: strong enough to attack steep finishing ramp but crash with Wright's knee contact raises durability questions for final week
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: proved sprint capability on 8.3% gradient ramps, unlike pure sprinter Groves who punctured, confirming hardman classification advantage
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: neutral: positioned well for uphill finish but knee contact caused Roglic crash, showing sprint chaos risk on ramped finishes
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: followed Roglic's acceleration on steep ramp showing adaptation beyond traditional flat sprint profile
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: punctured before steep finish suggesting equipment or positioning vulnerability when power surges begin on ramps
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: late puncture without team protection reveals DSM squad weakness in critical final kilometers despite time neutralization
